name: javasnoop
title: javasnoop

commands.name: javasnoop
commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# javasnoop -h
 [2] Turning off Java security for JavaSnoop usage
 [3] Starting JavaSnoop...
 [4] Turning Java security back on for safe browsing

binary_metadata.file_type: a sh script, ASCII text executable

apt_metadata.description:
Intercept Java applications locally
Normally, without access to the original source code,
testing the security of a Java client is unpredictable at
best and unrealistic at worst. With access the original
source, you can run a simple Java program and attach a
debugger to it remotely, stepping through code and changing
variables where needed. Doing the same with an applet is a
little bit more difficult.
JavaSnoop attempts to solve this problem by allowing you
attach to an existing process (like a debugger) and
instantly begin tampering with method calls, run custom
code, or just watch what's happening on the system.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use javasnoop because ### javasnoop.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair javasnoop with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `javasnoop --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: ### javasnoop.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install javasnoop
Install with: sudo apt install javasnoop
